title: Moroccan Smoked Salmon Tagine
description: A light and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring smoked salmon, shio-cure, and a blend of aromatic spices, perfect for a special occasion or a cozy dinner
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the olive oil, onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the smoked salmon to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Transfer the salmon mixture to a tagine or a large oven-safe dish with a tight-fitting lid.
Cover the dish with the lid and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the salmon is cooked through and the top is lightly browned.
Remove the dish from the oven and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
